name: Wedding / Event Vendor Intake: Deposits, Refund Clauses, Cancellation
description: Place an intake / inquiry call with a wedding or event vendor (venue, photographer, caterer, florist, DJ, planner, officiant) to confirm availability, get a realistic price range, surface deposit / refund / force majeure / cancellation terms, and request a written contract BEFORE any money moves.
disclaimer: Wedding-vendor deposits are the highest-volume source of contract disputes in this category. Never wire, Zelle, or charge a deposit on this call. All commitments are deferred to ask_operator after written-contract review.

context.when_to_use: Operator is sourcing vendors for a wedding or large event. Use one call per vendor early in the process. Date-blocking is the leverage point — popular vendors release dates on a first-deposit basis, so the operator's clarity on terms matters BEFORE the date is held.
preconditions: Operator has the event date (preferably with one or two alternates), location, and guest count.
Operator has a clear budget range for this specific vendor category.
Operator has a 2-3 sentence description of the event style and any vendor-specific specifics (cuisine style for catering, photo style for photographer, etc.).
Operator has identified at least 2-3 vendors to compare so this isn't a one-quote engagement.

principles: Date availability is the gating question. Without an open date, fit and price are irrelevant.
Wedding contracts skew vendor-friendly. Deposits are typically non-refundable; the operator's leverage is at the PRE-signature stage, not after.
Pricing varies seasonally and by day of week. Friday and Sunday rates are often 20-40% below Saturday. Off-season can be 30%+ lower. Mention flexibility if the operator has it.
Force majeure / postponement language is now standard post-2020 but inconsistently written. Pin down what triggers a free reschedule vs a forfeited deposit.
Vendor scope creep is the second-biggest cost driver. Travel, overtime, setup, breakdown, vendor meals, parking — surface every add-on category before signing.
References from the operator's specific venue or planner often matter more than star ratings. Ask for them.

phrases.opener: Hi — I'm calling for a client planning a wedding on [date] in [location], roughly [guest count] guests. Quick first question before anything else: are you available that date, and at a high level what's your deposit and cancellation policy?
phrases.date_probe: Are you available on [date], and what about [alt date 1] and [alt date 2] as backups? And is there a price difference by day of week?
phrases.pricing_probe: For an event of this size and style, what's a realistic baseline package, and what's the typical all-in including the common add-ons — travel, overtime, setup, meals, gratuity?
phrases.deposit_probe: What's the deposit, when is it due, is it refundable, and what's the payment schedule for the remainder?
phrases.cancellation_probe: What's the cancellation policy at 90 days, 60 days, 30 days, and 7 days out? Is the deposit forfeited, are partial refunds tiered, and are there fees beyond the deposit?
phrases.postponement_probe: If we postpone due to [force majeure event — illness, weather, venue loss], can we reschedule without forfeiting the deposit? What's the window — 6 months, 12 months, 18 months?
phrases.scope_probe: What's explicitly in the package, and what's commonly billed separately — overtime hours, additional staff, travel, parking, vendor meals, equipment rentals, model releases, raw files, edits?
phrases.team_probe: Who actually shows up on the day — you personally, a team you employ, or freelancers? If a backup, who, and is there an additional fee for the operator's first-choice point person?
phrases.insurance_probe: Do you carry liability insurance, and can you provide a Certificate of Insurance naming the venue as additional insured if required?
phrases.references_probe: Could you share two recent events at [venue / same planner / similar style] completed in the last 12-18 months — with contacts the operator can reach?
phrases.hold_probe: If the operator wants to proceed, can you provide a 5-7 day soft hold on the date while we review the written contract?
phrases.stall_to_check: Let me run the deposit and cancellation terms past my client before we lock anything — I'm not going to put money down on this call.
phrases.graceful_close: Thanks — could you email the written contract over with the deposit, cancellation, postponement, and an itemised package? My client will review and get back to you within [timeframe].

tactics.name: Date first, always
tactics.script: Use `date_probe`. If the primary date is gone and the operator has no flex, end politely and move on. Don't waste either side's time.

tactics.name: Baseline + add-ons
tactics.script: Use `pricing_probe` and `scope_probe`. The baseline package is rarely the operator's all-in number. Get the typical add-ons named so the comparison across vendors is apples-to-apples.

tactics.name: Deposit and cancellation explicit
tactics.script: Use `deposit_probe` and `cancellation_probe`. Wedding deposits are typically 25-50%. Cancellation grids range from 100% loss to tiered partial refunds. Get the numbers.

tactics.name: Postponement / force majeure
tactics.script: Use `postponement_probe`. Post-2020, vendors are more willing to write in a one-time free reschedule for force majeure within 12-18 months. Make sure it's in writing, not verbal.

tactics.name: Surface backups and team
tactics.script: Use `team_probe`. Photographers and DJs especially: who is the contracted person, who is the realistic backup, and what fee structure applies if the backup is sent.

tactics.name: Venue-required insurance
tactics.script: Use `insurance_probe`. Many venues require COI with additional-insured language. Surface this early so a chosen vendor can comply.

tactics.name: Real references at comparable venues
tactics.script: Use `references_probe`. Two recent events at the same venue or similar style beats 500 Instagram followers.

tactics.name: Soft hold while reviewing
tactics.script: Use `hold_probe`. A 5-7 day soft hold lets the operator review the contract without losing the date. Reasonable vendors offer this.

boundaries: Do NOT pay a deposit, retainer, or 'date hold fee' mid-call. NO exceptions. Use ask_operator.
Do NOT provide credit card, Zelle, Venmo, CashApp, or bank-transfer details to anyone on this call. Vendor payments go through the operator and an audited channel only.
Do NOT sign a contract or e-sign a 'simplified booking form' on the call. Every wedding contract requires offline review.
Do NOT accept a verbal-only quote. Wedding pricing is famously creative — written quote or it didn't happen.
Do NOT agree to a contract without force-majeure / postponement language the operator has approved.
Do NOT release the operator's full address, parents' contact info, or co-host details before booking. Vendor side-channel mailing is a known nuisance.
Do NOT commit to vendor meal counts, parking allocation, or overtime authority without the operator's say-so.
success_signals: Date availability confirmed with day-of-week pricing notes.
Written baseline package with add-ons enumerated as line items.
Deposit, cancellation grid, and postponement window explicit and in writing.
Force-majeure clause covers a sensible window (12-18 months reschedule).
Team / backup plan is clear, with named primary and backup.
Vendor will issue COI if the venue requires it.
Soft hold offered for 5-7 days while contract is under review.
failure_signals: Pressure to deposit on the call to 'lock the date'.
Deposit non-refundable AND no postponement clause at all.
Verbal-only quote with promise to 'figure out the contract later'.
No backup plan for sole-proprietor vendors (photographer / officiant) who could be sick on the day.
Refusal to provide references at comparable venues.
Aggressive add-on disclosure ('that's billed separately, but everyone pays it').
Refusal to write a force-majeure clause post-2020 norms.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm vendor will email: written contract, deposit and cancellation grid, postponement clause, itemised package and common add-ons, references, COI if needed, and a soft hold confirmation. Operator reviews and decides within the soft-hold window.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Politely close. If terms are the issue, ask if they have a counterpart in the same network whose contract terms are more reasonable. Report back with the specific deal-breaker for the operator.
follow_ups: Email the operator: vendor name, package, deposit / cancellation terms, postponement clause, add-on totals, references, red/green flags, comparison vs other quotes.
Calendar a hard deadline 24-48 hours BEFORE the soft hold expires to ensure the operator decides.
If proceeding: a separate contract-review pass before any signature.

required_user_info: Venue (city, name if booked) and guest count
Vendor category and a 2-3 sentence style description
Budget range for this vendor specifically
Operator's tolerance for non-Saturday or off-season dates (price leverage)
Operator's preferred communication channel and decision timeline
Names and contacts to keep private vs share with vendor
